sentence: O tempo não está nada bom hoje.
ipa: ˈo ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈʃta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ ˈɔʒɨ
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Article 'o' kept as back [o] (no eastern/western /u/ labialisation) per mikolajczak2014; reduction and nasals inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Um quilo de fruta custa dois euros.
ipa: ˈũ ˈkilu ˈdɨ ˈfɾutɐ ˈkuʃtɐ ˈdojz ˈewɾuʃ
gloss_en: A kilo of fruit costs two euros.
notes: Back [u] kept in 'fruta'/'custa' — Terceira does not front the stressed /u/ per mikolajczak2014; coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A reunião é depois do almoço.
ipa: ˈɐ ʁewniˈɐ̃w̃ ˈɛ dɨˈpwojʒ ˈdu ɐˈɫmwɔsu
gloss_en: The meeting is after lunch.
notes: Labial crescent on-glide [w] in 'depois'/'almoço' per mikolajczak2014; nasal [ɐ̃w̃], strong [ʁ] and dark coda [ɫ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: A minha família mora perto do mar.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈmwiɲɐ fɐˈmwiliɐ ˈmwɔɾɐ ˈpɛɾtu ˈdu ˈmaɾ
gloss_en: My family lives near the sea.
notes: Crescent [w] after labial onsets in 'minha'/'família'/'mora' per mikolajczak2014; palatal [ɲ] inherited per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Segue em frente e vira na segunda rua.
ipa: ˈsɨɡɨ ˈẽ ˈfɾẽtɨ ˈe ˈvwiɾɐ ˈnɐ ˈsɨɡndɐ ˈʁuɐ
gloss_en: Keep straight ahead and turn on the second street.
notes: Crescent [w] in 'vira'; back [u] kept in 'rua' (no fronting) per mikolajczak2014; 'segue em' liaison per mateus_dandrade2000; imperative register.

sentence: A sopa de peixe estava muito boa.
ipa: ˈɐ ˈsɔpɐ ˈdɨ ˈpejʃ eˈʃtavɐ ˈmujtu ˈbwɔɐ
gloss_en: The fish soup was very good.
notes: a-metaphony [a] raised to [ɔ] before a final -o in 'boa' per segura_saramago2001; 'ei' kept in 'peixe' and coda [ʃ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Onde é que apanho o autocarro?
ipa: ˈõdɨ ˈɛ ˈkɨ ɐˈpɔɲu ˈo awtuˈkɔʁu
gloss_en: Where do I catch the bus?
notes: a-metaphony in 'apanho' ([ɐˈpɔɲu]) and 'autocarro' ([awtuˈkɔʁu]) per segura_saramago2001; palatal [ɲ] and strong [ʁ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Quantas pessoas vêm ao jantar?
ipa: ˈkɐ̃tɐʃ pɨˈs̺ɔɐʒ ˈvẽ ˈɐu ʒɐ̃ˈtaɾ
gloss_en: How many people are coming to dinner?
notes: Nasal [ɐ̃]/[ẽ] and coda [ʃ] in 'quantas'; interrogative per mateus_dandrade2000; central-group profile per vasconcelos1892.

sentence: Se pudesse, comprava um gato preto.
ipa: ˈsɨ puˈdɛs̺ɨ kõˈpɾavɐ ˈũ ˈɡɔtu ˈpɾɛtu
gloss_en: If I could, I would buy a black cat.
notes: a-metaphony [a] raised to [ɔ] in 'gato' ([ˈɡɔtu]) per segura_saramago2001; open [ɛ] in 'preto', nasal in 'um' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Comprei um pato e queijo na feira.
ipa: kõˈpɾej ˈũ ˈpɔtu ˈe ˈkejʒu ˈnɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: I bought a duck and cheese at the market.
notes: a-metaphony in 'pato' ([ˈpɔtu]) per segura_saramago2001; 'ei' diphthongs in 'comprei'/'queijo'/'feira' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Amanhã vamos de barco até à ilha.
ipa: ɐmɐˈɲã ˈvɔmuʒ ˈdɨ ˈbaɾku ɐˈtɛ ˈa ˈiʎɐ
gloss_en: Tomorrow we go by boat to the island.
notes: a-metaphony in 'vamos' ([ˈvɔmuʒ]) per segura_saramago2001; palatal [ʎ] in 'ilha' and 'até à' liaison per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Telefona-me quando chegares a casa.
ipa: tɨlɨˈfwɔnɐ ˈmɨ ˈkɐ̃du ʃɨˈɡaɾɨz ˈɐ ˈkazɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: Crescent [w] in 'telefona'; nasal [ɐ̃] in 'quando' and coda [ʃ] in 'chegares' per mikolajczak2014; clitic imperative per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Boa tarde, então como tem passado?
ipa: ˈbwɔɐ ˈtaɾd ẽˈtɐ̃w̃ ˈkɔmu ˈtẽ pɐˈs̺ɔdu
gloss_en: Good afternoon, so how have you been?
notes: Crescent [w] in 'boa' and a-metaphony in 'passado' ([pɐˈs̺ɔdu]) per mikolajczak2014; nasal [ɐ̃w̃] in 'então' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois a voar.
ipa: ˈmajʒ ˈvalɨ ˈũ ˈpas̺ɐɾu ˈnɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ ˈkɨ ˈdojz ˈɐ vuˈaɾ
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Coda [ʃ] in 'mais'/'dois' and nasal [ɐ̃w̃] in 'mão' per mateus_dandrade2000; proverb frame; central-group profile per vasconcelos1892.

sentence: O carro do meu primo é muito rápido.
ipa: ˈo ˈkɔʁu ˈdu ˈmew ˈpɾimu ˈɛ ˈmujtu ˈʁapidu
gloss_en: My cousin's car is very fast.
notes: a-metaphony in 'carro' ([ˈkɔʁu]) per segura_saramago2001; strong [ʁ] and open [ɛ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: Naquela noite o porco fugiu do curral.
ipa: nɐˈkɛlɐ ˈnojtɨ ˈo ˈpwɔɾku fuˈɡiw ˈdu kuˈʁaɫ
gloss_en: That night the pig ran off from the pen.
notes: Crescent [w] in 'porco' ([ˈpwɔɾku]), the most individualising terceirense trait, per mikolajczak2014; dark coda [ɫ] in 'curral'.

sentence: O sol brilha por cima do mar azul.
ipa: ˈo ˈsɔɫ ˈbɾiʎɐ ˈpwɔɾ ˈsimɐ ˈdu ˈmaɾ ɐˈzuɫ
gloss_en: The sun shines over the blue sea.
notes: Crescent [w] in 'por', velar coda [ɫ] in 'sol'/'azul' and palatal [ʎ] in 'brilha' per mikolajczak2014; open [ɔ] per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O trabalho do meu filho é difícil.
ipa: ˈo tɾɐˈbɔʎu ˈdu ˈmew ˈfwiʎu ˈɛ diˈfwisiɫ
gloss_en: My son's job is difficult.
notes: a-metaphony and crescent [w] in 'trabalho' ([tɾɐˈbɔʎu]) and 'filho' per segura_saramago2001; dark coda [ɫ] in 'difícil' per mateus_dandrade2000.

sentence: O café está muito quente e forte.
ipa: ˈo kɐˈfɛ eˈʃta ˈmujtu ˈkẽtɨ ˈe ˈfwɔɾtɨ
gloss_en: The coffee is very hot and strong.
notes: Crescent [w] in 'forte'; open [ɛ] in 'café', nasal [ẽ] in 'quente' and coda [ʃ] per mikolajczak2014.

sentence: O bicho do mato assustou o gato.
ipa: ˈo ˈbwiʃu ˈdu ˈmɔtu ɐs̺uˈʃto ˈo ˈɡɔtu
gloss_en: The creature from the woods scared the cat.
notes: Crescent [w] in 'bicho' ([ˈbwiʃu]) and a-metaphony in 'mato'/'gato' ([ˈmɔtu]/[ˈɡɔtu]) per mikolajczak2014; 'ou' in 'assustou' per mateus_dandrade2000.
